name: Sales Discovery Call
description: For early-stage sales calls focused on understanding a prospect's needs, budget, and buying process - distinct from ongoing client/account meetings.

sections.title: Prospect & Meeting Context
sections.instruction: State the prospect/company name, attendees' roles, and the purpose of the call (e.g. first discovery call, follow-up demo).

sections.title: Attendees
sections.instruction: List attendees and their roles or companies

sections.title: Needs & Pain Points
sections.instruction: Describe the problems, pain points, or goals the prospect described in their own words. Focus on what's actually costing them time, money, or risk today.

sections.title: Current Solution / Status Quo
sections.instruction: Summarize what the prospect currently uses or does instead, and its shortcomings, if discussed.

sections.title: Budget & Decision Process
sections.instruction: Capture any budget figures, approval process, or decision-makers mentioned. If not discussed, say so explicitly - do not guess.

sections.title: Timeline
sections.instruction: State any timeline or urgency the prospect mentioned for solving this problem or making a decision.

sections.title: Objections & Concerns
sections.instruction: List any hesitations, objections, or concerns the prospect raised.

sections.title: Next Steps
sections.instruction: List agreed next steps, including the speaker's own first-person commitments (e.g. "I'll send over..."), owned by "Me".
